input.draft_text:
SEO Insertions on 3 Riders Which Are Best Suitable For A Child Life Insurance Policy - Article Page
Keyword density and no. of target keywords has been mentioned as comments in the beginning
Suggested additional anchor texts are marked as comments in Yellow. (If required)
Body Content:
Being a parent, it is natural for you to want to be able to make sure that your children’s dreams are fulfilled no matter what. One of the best ways to ensure that would be through a child life insurance policy.
A child plan provides both death benefits and maturity benefits, making sure that your child gets their dreams satisfied irrespective of what life has in store for you.
One of the primary highlights of a child insurance plan is that it can be customised according to your and your child’s needs by opting for riders. If you’re planning on purchasing a child plan in the near future, here are three riders that you can consider getting.
What is a Rider?
But before we actually go ahead and take a look at what the riders are, let’s take a detour and quickly see what the concept of riders are all about.
Riders are essentially add-ons that you can opt for at the time of purchase of an insurance plan. They provide additional benefits over and above what’s listed in the base plan for a nominal premium increase.
Top 3 Child Plan Riders
Now that you’ve seen what riders are, let’s get back to the main part of the article. The three riders that you should seriously consider opting for your child plan are -
Waiver of Premium Rider
This rider helps reduce the financial burden of the family by waiving off all future premium payments in the case of premature death of the policyholder, on the diagnosis of any one of the 11 specified critical illnesses, and accidental permanent total disability. By waiving off all future premium payments, this rider helps protect your family’s finances if any one of the above-mentioned insured events were to happen.
Accidental Death Benefit Rider
Life can be extremely unpredictable. Accidents can still happen even if you’re driving in a careful and cautious manner. The accidental death benefit rider provides the nominees with an additional sum assured in the case of the policyholder’s death due to an accident. This benefit would be over and above the death benefit sum assured listed under the base life insurance plan. The additional sum assured amount can be used by the policyholder’s family to provide for the children’s future.
Family Income Benefit Rider
The family income benefit rider ensures that the deceased policyholder’s nominees receive a portion of the rider sum assured each month throughout the rider term. This add-on ensures that the family receives a steady source of income, which they can use to take care of their monthly expenses. The benefit under this rider is also applicable if the policyholder faces an accidental total permanent disability or is diagnosed with any one of the 11 specified critical illnesses as well.
Conclusion
As you can see, waiver of premium, accidental death benefit, and family income riders are three of the most beneficial add-ons that you can opt for a child insurance policy. Although you would be required to pay a nominal additional premium for these riders, the benefits that they offer far outweigh their costs.

output.final_text:
3 Riders Which Are Suitable for a Child Life Insurance Policy Being a parent, it is natural for you to want to be able to make sure that your children’s dreams are fulfilled no matter what. One of the preferred ways to ensure that would be through a child life insurance policy. A child plan provides both death benefits and maturity benefits as per the policy terms & conditions, making sure that your child gets their dreams satisfied irrespective of what life has in store for you. One of the primary highlights of a child insurance plan is that it can be customised according to your and your child’s needs by opting for riders. If you’re planning on purchasing a child plan soon, here are two riders that you may consider getting. What is a Rider? Before we go ahead and look at what riders you can consider getting, let’s take a detour and quickly see what the concept of riders are all about. Riders are essentially add-ons that you can opt for at the time of purchase of an insurance plan. They provide additional benefits over and above what’s listed in the base plan for a nominal extra cost. 2 Child Plan Riders Now that you’ve seen what riders are, let’s get back to the main part of the article. The two riders that you can consider opting for your child plan are - 1. Waiver of Premium Rider This rider relieves the financial burden on the family by waiving all future premium payments in the event of the policyholder's premature death, the diagnosis of any of the critical illnesses listed in the product, or the accidental permanent total disability or any other conditions listed in the rider. This rider helps preserve your family's finances by waiving all future premium payments. The benefit under the insurance will stay the same since the child will be the nominee, and whether you invested for education or marriage, the maturity benefit will be provided to satisfy your life objectives as per the rider terms & conditions. 2. Accidental Death Benefit Rider Life can be extremely unpredictable. Accidents can still happen even if you’re driving in a careful and cautious manner. The accidental death benefit rider provides the nominees with an additional sum assured in the case of the policyholder’s death due to an accident. This benefit would be over and above the death benefit sum assured listed under the base life insurance plan. The additional sum assured amount can be used by the policyholder’s nominees to provide for the children’s future.
Conclusion As you can see, waiver of premium, and accidental death benefitare two beneficial add-ons that you may opt for in a child insurance policy. Although you would be required to pay a nominal additional premium for these riders, the benefits that they offer outweigh their costs.
